title: Build a Real-World Multi-Agent AI App with MCP + LangGraph | Part 2
summary: Tutorial showing how to build a multi-agent travel planning system by integrating LangGraph with MCP servers for real-time flight and weather data. The system combines LangGraph agents, Model Context Protocol servers, PostgreSQL for memory, and Streamlit for UI, demonstrating production-style agentic AI application development.
writeup: Part 2 of a Code With Aarohi tutorial builds a multi-agent travel planning system by wiring LangGraph agents to Model Context Protocol servers that supply real-time flight and weather data. The stack pairs those agents and MCP servers with PostgreSQL for memory and Streamlit for the interface, illustrating a production-style agentic AI build. The concrete takeaways: how to integrate LangGraph with MCP servers, how to persist agent memory in PostgreSQL, and how to surface the result through a Streamlit UI.
